output.text:
To solve the problem, we first need to understand the geometric configuration of the four circles and the triangle. The circles are tangent to each other and also tangent to the sides of the triangle \( \triangle ABC \). Each circle has a radius of 1, so the centers of the circles are 2 units apart (since the radius is 1 and the circles are tangent). ки
The centers of the circles are denoted as \( P, Q, R, \) and \( S \). We need to determine the angles in the triangle \( \triangle PQS \). ки
First, observe that the centers \( P, Q, R, \) and \( S \) form a square with side length 2 (since the distance between the centers of any two adjacent circles is 2). This is because each circle has a radius of 1, and the distance between the centers of two adjacent circles is the sum of their radii, which is 2. ки
Now, consider the triangle \( \triangle PQS \). Since \( P, Q, \) and \( S \) form a square, the triangle \( \triangle PQS \) is a right triangle with the right angle at \( P \). The side lengths of \( \triangle PQS \) are the same as the distances between the centers of the circles, which are 2, 2, and 2. Therefore, \( \triangle PQS \) is an isosceles right triangle with the legs of length 2. ки
The angle at the right angle in an isosceles right triangle is \( 90^\circ \). Therefore, the smallest angle in \( \triangle PQS \) is \( 90^\circ \). ки
Thus, the degree measure of the smallest angle in triangle \( \triangle PQS \) is \(\boxed{90}\). ки
